Which pair of subfields does it bridge? Only the mechanism genome field is shown.

Genome:
  Mechanism: "The paper derives geometric and torsional rules for cross-β motif formation using structured β-arch loops and helices, then applies Rosetta-based backbone assembly, ABEGO-guided loop selection, hydrogen-bond constraints, sequence design, and folding-based filtering to build 7-stranded Ig domains."

A. AI辅助抗体设计 ↔ 地震多次波衰减
B. AI辅助抗体设计 ↔ 台风风压关系
C. AI辅助抗体设计 ↔ Neural PDE Solvers and Scientific Computing
D. Neural PDE Solvers and Scientific Computing ↔ q-fin.GN
E. AI Drug Discovery ↔ AI辅助抗体设计
F. Neural PDE Solvers and Scientific Computing ↔ q-fin.PM
G. AI辅助抗体设计 ↔ 地震波检测
H. AI辅助抗体设计 ↔ 引力波检测与参数估计(GW Detection & Parameter Estimation)

Part 1: Select the subfield pair (A-H)
  Contribution type: method | dataset | analysis | system | theory
  Dynamics of the genome's lineage context: Mutation | Adaptive Radiation | Hybridization | Speciation | Niche Competition

--- Dynamics Label Definitions ---
Mutation: Direct inheritance with local modification of the driver gene.
Adaptive Radiation: The inherited mechanism is applied to a shifted problem niche.
Hybridization: The driver gene combines elements from 2+ distinct lineages.
Speciation: A new lineage-founding mechanism replaces the predecessor's approach.
Niche Competition: Papers target the same niche without inheriting the driver gene.

Key distinction — Mutation requires that the successor's mechanism is clearly derived from (extends/refines) the predecessor's mechanism. If mechanisms are fundamentally different approaches even for a related problem, classify as Speciation.
Hybridization vs Speciation: if the successor's mechanism combines recognizable elements from the predecessor with ideas from another lineage, use Hybridization. Speciation means the mechanism shares no ancestry with the predecessor.
Speciation vs Niche Competition: Speciation means the successor IS in the same lineage but replaced the mechanism. Niche Competition means no lineage relationship — independent work targeting the same niche.

Answer with exactly one line:
Label = [A|B|C|D|E|F|G|H]

choices.B: AI辅助抗体设计 ↔ 台风风压关系
choices.C: AI辅助抗体设计 ↔ Neural PDE Solvers and Scientific Computing
choices.D: Neural PDE Solvers and Scientific Computing ↔ q-fin.GN
choices.E: AI Drug Discovery ↔ AI辅助抗体设计
choices.F: Neural PDE Solvers and Scientific Computing ↔ q-fin.PM

Part 1: Select the subfield pair (A-H)
  Contribution type: method | dataset | analysis | system | theory
  Dynamics of the genome's lineage context: Mutation | Adaptive Radiation | Hybridization | Speciation | Niche Competition

--- Dynamics Label Definitions ---
Mutation: Direct inheritance with local modification of the driver gene.
Adaptive Radiation: The inherited mechanism is applied to a shifted problem niche.
Hybridization: The driver gene combines elements from 2+ distinct lineages.
Speciation: A new lineage-founding mechanism replaces the predecessor's approach.
Niche Competition: Papers target the same niche without inheriting the driver gene.

Key distinction — Mutation requires that the successor's mechanism is clearly derived from (extends/refines) the predecessor's mechanism. If mechanisms are fundamentally different approaches even for a related problem, classify as Speciation.
Hybridization vs Speciation: if the successor's mechanism combines recognizable elements from the predecessor with ideas from another lineage, use Hybridization. Speciation means the mechanism shares no ancestry with the predecessor.
Speciation vs Niche Competition: Speciation means the successor IS in the same lineage but replaced the mechanism. Niche Competition means no lineage relationship — independent work targeting the same niche.

Answer with exactly one line:
Label = [A|B|C|D|E|F|G|H]

  Contribution type: method | dataset | analysis | system | theory
  Dynamics of the genome's lineage context: Mutation | Adaptive Radiation | Hybridization | Speciation | Niche Competition

--- Dynamics Label Definitions ---
Mutation: Direct inheritance with local modification of the driver gene.
Adaptive Radiation: The inherited mechanism is applied to a shifted problem niche.
Hybridization: The driver gene combines elements from 2+ distinct lineages.
Speciation: A new lineage-founding mechanism replaces the predecessor's approach.
Niche Competition: Papers target the same niche without inheriting the driver gene.

Key distinction — Mutation requires that the successor's mechanism is clearly derived from (extends/refines) the predecessor's mechanism. If mechanisms are fundamentally different approaches even for a related problem, classify as Speciation.
Hybridization vs Speciation: if the successor's mechanism combines recognizable elements from the predecessor with ideas from another lineage, use Hybridization. Speciation means the mechanism shares no ancestry with the predecessor.
Speciation vs Niche Competition: Speciation means the successor IS in the same lineage but replaced the mechanism. Niche Competition means no lineage relationship — independent work targeting the same niche.

Answer with exactly one line:
Label = [A|B|C|D|E|F|G|H]
